name: BSL Platform Context MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing 1C:Enterprise BSL (Business Script Language) platform context to AI agents — exposing the 1C platform API reference, object model, built-in functions, and development context to enable AI-assisted development for 1C:Enterprise applications. Primarily useful for 1C/BSL developers in Russia and CIS countries building business automation on the 1C platform.
use_cases: AI-assisted 1C:Enterprise BSL code completion from 1C development agents
Querying 1C platform API reference from code assistant agents
Understanding 1C platform object model from architecture agents
Generating BSL code snippets for 1C configurations from productivity agents
Validating 1C API usage against platform context from code review agents
Onboarding developers to 1C platform with AI guidance from training agents
not_for: Teams outside Russia/CIS where 1C:Enterprise is uncommon
General ERP development (use SAP, Oracle, or other ERP MCPs for those platforms)
best_when: A 1C:Enterprise developer needs AI assistance with BSL code — this provides platform context (API, object model) that enables more accurate AI code suggestions for the 1C ecosystem.
avoid_when: Your team doesn't use 1C:Enterprise — this is a highly specialized tool for the Russian/CIS enterprise software ecosystem.

auth.notes: No authentication — reads local 1C platform context files. No external service required. 1C platform installation or context export may be needed.

pricing.notes: Free open source MCP. 1C:Enterprise itself requires commercial license from 1C company.

performance.rate_limits: Local — no external rate limits; context lookup is fast

known_agent_gotchas: Documentation primarily in Russian — English-speaking developers may struggle with setup
1C:Enterprise platform context requires specific platform version — ensure context matches your 1C version
Very niche tool — primarily useful for Russian/CIS 1C:Enterprise developers
BSL code generation quality depends on LLM familiarity with 1C syntax (rare in general LLMs)
1C platform has Russian-language API names by default — code may use Cyrillic identifiers
Community MCP maintained by 1c-syntax org — check for active maintenance
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local context files only. No external calls. No credentials. 1C platform context (API reference) is not sensitive. Safe for use in development environments.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local-only. No network calls. No credentials. Platform context reference data — not sensitive.
